domain: AI agent orchestration — TypeScript-native framework with visual canvas
product_type: Open-source framework with commercial freemium SaaS cloud platform
product_vision: Crewspace — The TypeScript-native agent orchestration framework with a beautiful visual canvas. Build, debug, and deploy multi-agent workflows in under 5 minutes. Open-source core (MIT license) drives community adoption; commercial cloud platform (Free → Pro $25/user/mo → Team $49/user/mo → Enterprise custom) drives revenue. OSS-first strategy: framework (M1-2) → visual canvas + debugging (M3-4) → templates + chat-to-workflow + team features (M5-8) → growth + enterprise pipeline (M9-12). Primary moat is OSS community + TypeScript-native DX. Year 1 target: $100-200K ARR on $0 investment.
target_audience: Primary: PMs blocked on engineering (200K-500K globally) and founders in idea stage (500K-1M). Secondary: developers evaluating agent frameworks (100K-300K). Developer-first GTM — no enterprise sales motion in Year 1.
monetization: Freemium SaaS: Free tier (500 runs/month, 5 agents) → Pro $25/user/month → Team $49/user/month (3-seat minimum) → Enterprise custom. Year 1 baseline: $100-200K ARR (300-600 paying users). 1-2% free-to-paid conversion rate.
owner_notes: VISION: Make agent orchestration accessible to a broader audience — just like Lovable/v0 democratized coding, do the same for multi-agent systems. Users should feel empowered to configure their own 'AI company' of specialized agents working together. INSPIRATION: Think Lovable's UX quality meets Figma's visual design meets Linear's polish. The company metaphor (hire agents, set directives, watch collaboration) should make complex orchestration intuitive. RESEARCH FIRST: Study competitors (Lovable, Base44, v0, Bolt, Replit Agent, CrewAI, AutoGen, LangGraph, Multi-On) and identify what makes orchestration hard today. Find the real pain points through user research before committing to specific features or tech choices. CREATIVE FREEDOM: The specific feature set, tech stack, and approach are for you to research and propose. The above is directional — not prescriptive.
strategic_questions_to_research: What makes existing agent frameworks inaccessible? Is it complexity, UX, debugging, or something else?
Should this be visual-first (no-code) or code-optional (low-code with escape hatches)?
What's the right business model? Usage-based, seat-based, or hybrid?
How do we achieve Lovable-level UX quality for a more complex domain?
What's the MVP that provides immediate value while being technically achievable?
Which segment should we target first: non-developers, developers, or enterprises?
How do competitors monetize and what can we learn from their pricing?

must_haves: Real value within first 5 minutes of use
open_questions: Tech stack (research best options for real-time, visual canvas, agent runtime)
Specific features (research and validate with potential users)
Pricing model (research competitor pricing and willingness to pay)
Company name and brand identity (research naming conventions in the space)

must_research_topics: Complete competitive landscape analysis (existing agent platforms and no-code tools)
User pain points with current agent orchestration tools
Lovable/v0/Bolt UX patterns and what makes them successful
Multi-agent framework adoption rates and developer sentiment
Pricing models in the no-code/low-code and AI tools space
Technical architecture patterns for real-time collaborative platforms
Market size and growth trends for agent orchestration tools
